pi_items.source: Task description states: 'Create a new Saved Search in Juris-M named "Post-1960 SCOTUS Decisions"'. All four keywords are present in the task's stated search name.
Task description explicitly states 'decided after the year 1960'. Verifier checks for date conditions containing '1960'. Injected dataset contains cases from 1803-2015, allowing proper filtering.

All injected case items have court field set to 'United States Supreme Court' (contains 'Supreme'). SCOTUS is standard legal abbreviation for Supreme Court of the United States. U.S./US are standard reporter abbreviations for U.S. Reports (Supreme Court reporter). All keywords are authoritative legal terminology.

Task description requires filtering by (1) court 'United States Supreme Court' and (2) date 'decided after the year 1960'. Verifier.py implements check_date_condition() and check_court_condition(). Both conditions are mandatory for task completion.

pi_items.verified_value: Real US Supreme Court cases and law review articles
pi_items.source: inject_references.py contains 10 references: 6 real Supreme Court cases (Brown v. Board of Education [1954], Marbury v. Madison [1803], Miranda v. Arizona [1966], New York Times v. Sullivan [1964], Gideon v. Wainwright [1963], Obergefell v. Hodges [2015], Tinker v. Des Moines [1969]) and 3 real law journal articles (The Path of the Law [1897], Constitutional Fact Review [1985], Due Process and Substantive Tort Law [1971]). All are well-documented historical legal sources.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a Saved Search in Jurism with keywords Post-1960, SCOTUS, Supreme Court, and 1960 in the name. The search must apply two mandatory conditions: (1) a date condition filtering for cases after 1960, and (2) a court condition filtering for Supreme Court cases using keywords like 'Supreme', 'SCOTUS', 'U.S.', or 'US'. The task operates on a Jurism database pre-populated with 10 real legal references including well-known Supreme Court cases (Brown v. Board of Education from 1954, Miranda v. Arizona from 1966, Marbury v. Madison from 1803, and others) plus law review articles from Harvard, Columbia, and Yale Law Reviews. The verifier checks: search creation (count increase), name keyword presence, date condition logic (operator must be 'greater', 'isAfter', or 'is not before' with value 1960), court condition logic (field must be court/authority/publicationTitle with operator 'contains'/'is'/'beginsWith' and value containing Supreme/SCOTUS/U.S./US keywords), and VLM verification that the search appears in the sidebar.
